input_text: Emajogi's length is 80 kilometers. Sandra journeyed to the kitchen. The toy of the child was broken. Yellow and green elephants are nice. Mr Dursley is an American. A hand of a man moved a wheel. Gertrude is a wolf. Tallinn is a city. Riga is not in Estonia. The big mouse is strong. John works at the hospital every day. Mary decided to leave. The guide offered the tourists tea. The letter was written. Mike is male. Mark is not in the box. The brother of the friend of Eve arrived. Where does John work?

nl_proof: Explained:
Sentences used:
(1) John works at the hospital every day.
(2) Where does John work?
Proof steps:
(1) If X takes place some Z some Y and X is a work event and John performs X and X is typical and X is an activity then [Z, Y] is the answer  [sentence 2]
(2) The event act3 is a work event  [sentence 1]
(3) If the event act3 is an activity and the act3 takes place some Y some X and John performs the act3 and the act3 is typical then [Y, X] is the answer  [from steps 1, 2]
(4) John performs the event act3  [sentence 1]
(5) If the event act3 is an activity and the act3 takes place some Y3 some X3 and the act3 is typical then [Y3, X3] is the answer  [from steps 3, 4]
(6) The event act3 is an activity  [sentence 1]
(7) If the event act3 takes place some Y some X and the act3 is typical then [Y, X] is the answer  [from steps 5, 6]
(8) The event act3 takes place at the hospital B  [sentence 1]
(9) If the event act3 is typical then 'at the hospital B' is the answer  [from steps 7, 8]
(10) technical answer unwrap step  [from question]
(11) If the event act3 is typical then 'at the hospital B' is an answer  [from steps 9, 10]
(12) Normally the event act3 is typical  [sentence 1]
(13) 'At the hospital B' is an answer, except when the event act3 is not typical  [from steps 11, 12]
Exceptions checked and not holding:
act3 is not typical
